mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters. It simplifies the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to automate RabbitMQ deployments in Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install the operator using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator repository.
> This command updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages RabbitMQ clusters within your Kubernetes environment.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.19.2 --namespace rabbitmq-system --create-namespace

```

> This command installs the operator in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined configuration file.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> This command applies the example RabbitMQ cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system

```

> This command lists all pods in the rabbitmq-system namespace to verify their status.
> This command checks the status of RabbitMQ clusters managed by the operator.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if the RabbitMQ clusters are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq-system

```

> This command uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.19.2 --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Backup current RabbitMQ cluster configurations.
> This command upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the required permissions are not set for the operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the operator has the necessary RBAC permissions to create resources in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RabbitMQ cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RabbitMQ pods are not starting due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in your RabbitMQ cluster configuration and adjust them as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: conflict'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there is a version conflict with the existing RabbitMQ cluster resource.

**Fix:**
Use 'kubectl edit rabbitmqcluster <name> -n rabbitmq-system' to resolve conflicts manually.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to RabbitMQ'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the RabbitMQ service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure that it is accessible from your application.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved handling of RabbitMQ cluster upgrades.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with a status of 'Running' and the RabbitMQ cluster resource in 'Ready' state when everything is working correctly.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
